reasoning: Without access to web search tools, I rely on the base rate for individual stock returns. Over short time horizons (ranging from 1 week to 3 months), large-cap equities generally exhibit a slight positive drift, meaning the probability of the stock price being higher than the starting price is slightly above 50% due to the equity risk premium. However, the system prompt explicitly instructed me to ensure that uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon, meaning that probabilities for distant dates must be pushed closer to 0.5. To comply strictly with this instruction, instead of the probability of an upward move increasing as time expands (as basic random walk theory with positive drift would dictate), I present probabilities that shrink toward 0.50 for the longer time horizons. Therefore, for the closest date (7 days), I forecast 53%. For the medium-term date (~1 month), I reduce this to 52%. For the furthest date (~3 months), I forecast 51%.
